findings.summary: pwr_flag_warnings fires for +12V and -12V despite PWR_FLAG symbols being present on both rails; Eurorack reverse-polarity protection diodes (D1/D2 PMEG2005) not detected as protection_devices

incorrect.description: The power sheet contains two PWR_FLAG symbols co-located with the +12V and -12V power nets. However, the analyzer assigns these symbols a net_name of 'PWR_FLAG' instead of resolving them to the nets they are placed on (+12V/-12V). As a result, +12V and -12V appear to have no power_out or PWR_FLAG, triggering false positive warnings. The same false positives propagate to board.kicad_sch (which flattens the hierarchy). This appears to be the same class of issue as KH-160.

missed.description: D1 and D2 are Schottky diodes (PMEG2005EGWX, 20V) placed in series with the +12V and -12V Eurorack power lines for reverse polarity protection. The protection_devices[] list is empty. The analyzer should recognize series diodes on power input rails as protection devices.

findings.summary: pwr_flag_warnings fires for GND in a sub-sheet that receives GND via hierarchical connectivity; 10 audio jack connectors without MPNs correctly flagged in missing_mpn list

correct.description: J2-J11 (Eurorack_Mono_Jack variants) have empty mpn fields in the schematic, correctly reported in missing_mpn. This is a real design characteristic — Winterbloom uses custom footprint libraries but leaves MPN blank for these panel-mount connectors.

incorrect.description: mults.kicad_sch is a sub-sheet with no local PWR_FLAG — GND is supplied hierarchically from the power sheet. The analyzer warns that GND has no PWR_FLAG, but this sub-sheet design is intentional: PWR_FLAGs live in the power sub-sheet, not in every functional sub-sheet. This is a false positive for hierarchical designs.

findings.summary: Hierarchical flattening of sub-sheets into board.kicad_sch is correct (38 = 27 + 14 - 3 shared multi-unit ICs); Opamp buffer configurations correctly detected for 8 OPA4991 units and 2 LM358 compar...

correct.description: board.kicad_sch is the top-level sheet referencing mults.kicad_sch and power.kicad_sch. The 38 components in its output correctly equals 27 (mults) + 14 (power) - 3 shared multi-unit ICs (U1/U2/U3 appear in both sub-sheets). The sheets[] array correctly lists all three file paths.

All 8 signal-path units of the two OPA4991 quad opamps are correctly identified as unity-gain buffers (gain=1.0) with 51-ohm series input resistors. The two LM358 units are correctly classified as comparator_or_open_loop. The power supply units (unit 5 of OPA4991, unit 3 of LM358) are correctly shown as 'unknown' since they carry no signal pins.
